title: quassia praecox
intro: Quassia praecox, also known as the bitterwood or surinam toadroot, is a small, deciduous shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its bitter compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Quassia praecox include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve stomach discomfort, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Quassia praecox can be found in the form of tinctures, teas, and capsules, often used to treat various digestive issues and other health problems.
